label: Raspberry Pi - IoT Gateway (LoRa <-> MQTT)
info:
Pure communication gateway. No irrigation logic, no DB, no API, no auth.
Responsibilities:
 1) LoRa -> MQTT (sensor data relay) -> irrigation/abdellah/data (proper JSON, buffered + retried)
 2) MQTT -> LoRa (command relay) <- irrigation/abdellah/cmd
 3) Heartbeat monitoring -> irrigation/rpi/raspStatus (buffered + retried)
 4) Error handling -> irrigation/rpi/error

name: === SECTION 1: LoRa -> MQTT (Sensor Data Relay) ===
info: Receives raw LoRa packets from ESP32, filters by id='abdellah', normalizes fields into a clean JSON OBJECT, and publishes to MQTT broker (OVH) on irrigation/abdellah/data. Payload is sent as a real JSON object (msg.payload), so the MQTT out node serializes it properly - no double-encoded strings. Buffered + retried if MQTT is unreachable.

name: Filter & Parse (id=abdellah)
Normalize ESP32 Fields
Enqueue Sensor Data Msg
Flush Sensor Data Outbox (FIFO)
=== SECTION 2: MQTT -> LoRa (Command Relay) ===
info: Receives commands from OVH server via MQTT, converts them into the LoRa packet format expected by the ESP32, and transmits over the LoRa serial link. No validation of command semantics beyond basic JSON/format checks.

name: Validate & Format for LoRa
=== SECTION 3: Connection / Heartbeat Monitoring ===
info: Publishes a heartbeat to MQTT every 30 seconds containing gateway id, online status, uptime, and last_seen timestamp -> irrigation/rpi/raspStatus. Buffered (latest-only) + retried.

name: Build Heartbeat Payload
=== Buffer & Retry: Heartbeat -> irrigation/rpi/raspStatus ===
info: If MQTT broker is unreachable, only the LATEST heartbeat is kept (no stale-uptime spam) and flushed once connection returns or on the periodic retry.

name: Enqueue Heartbeat Msg
Flush Heartbeat Outbox
=== SECTION 5: Error Handling ===
info: Catches uncaught errors from any node on this tab and publishes them to a dedicated MQTT diagnostics topic.

name: Format Error Report
